headline: Survey at 22% completion; strong teacher engagement but regional and geographic gaps emerging.
collection_status: 299 of 1,350 responses collected (22% of target). Collection concentrated in 3 regions (Lindi, Mara, Tanga = 167 responses, 56% of total). Pace requires acceleration across remaining 7 regions, particularly Rukwa (5) and Manyara (4) which are critically underrepresented.
key_findings: Teacher respondents dominate (146 of 299, 49%), validating grassroots engagement; head teachers (73, 24%) provide institutional perspective but volunteers (31, 10%) and district officials (39, 13%) suggest weak system-level buy-in.

Expansion and new programs are top priorities (174 responses, 58%), indicating stakeholders see growth opportunity; however, scale readiness is low (2.7/5), signaling capacity constraints must be addressed before expansion.

Partner satisfaction strong at 4.4/5, but qualitative feedback reveals fragmented implementation: requests focus on incentives, digital monitoring, inter-stakeholder communication, and government policy integration—operational maturity gaps not reflected in satisfaction scores.
Regional imbalance risks skewed findings: Lindi + Mara represent 40% of responses but only 20% of target regions; Rukwa and Manyara near-absent, threatening representativeness.
flags: Collection pace (299 in current cycle) must accelerate 4.5x to meet 1,350 target by deadline; Rukwa and Manyara collection critical to avoid regional bias.
Scale readiness (2.7/5) contradicts expansion priority sentiment (110 responses); risk of committing to growth without operational foundation.

Qualitative feedback suggests implementation inconsistency (monitoring, incentives, communication) despite high satisfaction—potential measurement gap or satisfaction driven by engagement rather than outcomes.
recommendation: Immediately deploy targeted collection blitz in Rukwa, Manyara, and undersampled districts (Singida, Shinyanga); simultaneously commission rapid diagnostic on scale readiness barriers to align expansion ambitions with operational capacity. Both are critical to avoid biased findings and flawed strategy.
